envelope.useCase: Postpartum handoff from obstetric care to primary care after gestational diabetes mellitus

conditions.note: Pregnancy complicated by GDM requiring active monitoring during pregnancy.

obstetricHistory.historyNote: This is the first documented pregnancy and it was complicated by gestational diabetes mellitus.

keyObservations.note: Postpartum BMI remains relevant to metabolic follow-up planning.

keyResults.display: Oral glucose tolerance test in pregnancy
keyResults.note: Diagnostic pregnancy glucose result supporting the diagnosis of gestational diabetes mellitus.

medications.note: Pregnancy-related insulin treatment stopped after delivery.

documents.title: Postpartum discharge summary
documents.note: Contains discharge recommendations and postpartum follow-up instructions.

riskItems.display: Postpartum dysglycaemia and future diabetes risk after GDM
riskItems.basis: Confirmed gestational diabetes mellitus during pregnancy with abnormal glucose testing and pregnancy treatment history.
riskItems.clinicalMeaning: The woman remains at elevated risk of postpartum dysglycaemia and future type 2 diabetes, so follow-up must continue after discharge rather than ending with the birth episode.
riskItems.note: Risk should remain visible to primary care at the time of handoff.

riskInterpretation.riskSummary: Pregnancy was complicated by gestational diabetes mellitus. Although the pregnancy has ended, metabolic follow-up remains necessary in primary care because postpartum dysglycaemia and longer-term diabetes risk remain clinically relevant.

followUpItems.triggerReason: Prior gestational diabetes mellitus in the index pregnancy.
followUpItems.note: Primary care follow-up should include postpartum glucose testing within the expected postpartum follow-up window.

followUpItems.display: Primary care review of postpartum metabolic risk
followUpItems.triggerReason: Need to continue metabolic risk follow-up after obstetric discharge.
followUpItems.note: Responsibility for ongoing metabolic follow-up transfers to primary care after discharge.

followUpPlanAndResponsibility.responsibilitySummary: At discharge, obstetric care closes the birth-related episode, but responsibility for postpartum metabolic follow-up passes to primary care. The receiving primary care physician is expected to ensure postpartum glucose reassessment and ongoing risk-aware follow-up.

handoffContext.handoffReason: Postpartum discharge after pregnancy complicated by gestational diabetes mellitus, with need for ongoing metabolic follow-up in primary care.
handoffContext.handoffSummary: The woman is being discharged from obstetric care after delivery. Because the pregnancy was complicated by gestational diabetes mellitus, postpartum glucose follow-up and continued metabolic risk awareness must be preserved in primary care.

provenance.verificationNote: Package generated from the postpartum discharge context and reviewed by the responsible obstetric team.

narrativeSummary.summaryText: Woman discharged from obstetric care after delivery following a pregnancy complicated by gestational diabetes mellitus. Postpartum and longer-term metabolic follow-up remain necessary. Primary care is expected to arrange postpartum glucose reassessment and continue risk-aware follow-up after discharge.
